data.title: Nvidia Stock (NVDA) Rises Despite U.S. Push to Curb AI Chip Exports
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) shares were higher in pre-market today even as U.S. lawmakers pushed to block its AI chip exports to China and parts of Southeast Asia. The move follows a recent case where Nvidia chips were allegedly routed to China through third countries.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade NVDA with leverage
  • New trading tool for NVDA bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade NVDA

The push comes after the indictment of Super Micro Computer (SMCI) co-founder Wally Liaw, who is accused of bypassing U.S. export controls.

Lawmakers Call for Tighter Export Rules

Lawmakers pointed to the Super Micro case as evidence that current monitoring systems may not be strong enough. In a letter to the Commerce Department, U.S. senators urged officials to pause or suspend export licenses for advanced Nvidia chips, citing the risk that the technology could still reach China through intermediaries.

They also raised questions about earlier statements from Nvidia's CEO Jensen Huang, who had assured officials that customers "monitor themselves very carefully" to prevent illegal diversion. Lawmakers said the scale of the issue suggests gaps in monitoring and compliance.

In response, Nvidia said following export rules remains a top priority and that it continues to work with customers and the government. The company added that it does not provide support for systems that are diverted illegally.

What's Next for Investors?

This escalation comes just as the Trump administration had begun issuing licenses for Nvidia's H200 chips to China. At the same time, the House Foreign Affairs Committee is set to vote on the Chip Security Act this week, which could tighten controls further.

For investors, the key question is whether export rules will become stricter. Any change could affect Nvidia's access to key markets and its growth outlook in the near term.

Is NVDA Stock a Buy?

Nvidia's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 42 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 41 Buy and one Hold recommendations issued in the past three months. The average NVDA price target of $273.34 implies 56% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Beyond the Strait: The AI Power Grab Ignoring Iran War
data.description:
As the S&P 500 (SPX) retreated 5.5% this month, the global narrative has been dominated by a single, volatile geography. Following the February 28 United States and Israel joint strikes on Iran, the subsequent closure of the Strait of Hormuz sent Brent crude surging and ignited a broad "risk-off" sentiment across global equities. Yet, while the geopolitical floor was dropping out from under the markets, a significant corner of the American energy sector had escaped the market's gravitational pull.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On March 2, as the war in the Middle East intensified, the AES Corporation (AES), a large U.S.-based power company, saw its shares drop 17%. The war had nothing to do with it. The catalyst was a $33.4 billion definitive agreement to go private, led by a powerhouse consortium of BlackRock's (BLK) global infrastructure partner (GIP) — the infrastructure arm of BlackRock — and the Swedish private equity giant EQT (EQT).

While the war continues to cause havoc elsewhere, this deal has effectively anchored AES at a time when other utilities are sinking. This divergence highlights a massive "Artificial Intelligence (AI) Power Grab." For institutional giants, the long-term need for data center infrastructure is now a louder investment signal than the immediate thunder of global conflict.

Merger Arbitrage: The 12% Sanctuary

In a market currently defined by geopolitical volatility, the AES take-private has created what arbitrageurs call a "technical dislocation," a pocket of predictable return that is effectively blind to the conflict in the Middle East. This is where merger arbitrageurs step in, aiming to profit from the gap between the current market price and the final $15 buyout offer.

Currently, AES trades at a roughly 7% discount to that offer, yet it continues to provide a safety net to investors with a 5% annual dividend yield. For the "smart money," this represents a 12% potential return tied to the deal's completion rather than to Brent crude fluctuations.

In a typical take-private, the target's share price should converge toward the offer price, which is $15 in the case of AES. That AES trades at a discount is simply the market's way of pricing in the risk that the deal fails.

The trade seems simple: buy and hold, and get a cash payment at close. However, this sanctuary is not without risk. While the stock is largely decoupled from the Iran-Israel conflict, it remains hyper-sensitive to "deal-breaker" scenarios. If the deal doesn't close, investors may get hit. In our case, AES was trading at $10 before the deal was leaked, implying about 30% downside risk. Of course, one can buy put options, but this can kill the profit potential.

The Regulatory Risk

The risk with most deals is the complex regulatory path. The highest-risk hurdle is the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS), which reviews the national security implications of foreign investments in the U.S. economy. In the case of AES take-private, in addition to the lead partners — BlackRock's GIP and EQT — Qatar and CalPERS (the California public pension fund) are co-underwriters, providing a significant portion of the equity for the deal.

Evaluating a Qatari sovereign investor's role in U.S. critical energy infrastructure is a scenario that keeps CFIUS reviewers up at night, even if Qatar is also getting hit by the Iranian forces. The irony here is that Qatar's own liquefied natural gas (LNG) facilities at Ras Laffan were hit by Iranian drones on March 2, the very day the deal was announced.

Add to this the multiple state utility commissions and the countries in which AES operates, and the regulatory risk becomes more complex. AES owns regulated utilities serving 1.1 million customers in Ohio and Indiana, operates generation facilities critical to the U.S. grid, and holds contracts to power hyperscale data centers.

Dry Powder on Target

From a deal-making angle, the macro environment favors AES's assets, but it is the company's financial position that makes this deal a good outcome for shareholders. As I mentioned in prior columns, it goes back to the fundamentals. The AI hyperscalers, Microsoft (MSFT), Meta (META), Alphabet (GOOGL), need clean energy for their data center buildout. Moody's (MCO) and S&P (SPGI) rated AES Baa3/BBB, respectively, indicating the lowest possible "investment grade" scores. This is just a nudge above junk, making it difficult to fund the buildout as a public company without diluting existing shareholders.

The AES board admitted that without the take-private deal, the company would likely need to cut its dividend or issue equity at distressed prices. The consortium provides 100% equity financing with no incremental debt, matching the investment duration to the buildout.

For firms like GIP and EQT, "dry powder" is more than just a cash reserve. It is the fuel required to fund a clean energy buildout that the AES balance sheet could no longer support as a public entity. Prior to the deal leak, a research analyst had a $5 price target as recently as June 2025, citing a debt-to-equity ratio exceeding 8x. Jefferies (JEF) had downgraded the AES stock to Underperform. AES was selling assets defensively: its stake in AES Brasil and a 30% sell-down of AES Ohio to Caisse de dépôt et placement du Québec (CDPQ), driven by liquidity pressure.

One must add, though, that the buyers are far from philanthropists. They see 11.8 gigawatt of contracted clean energy supply to the world's largest hyperscalers at a compressed multiple, and they can fund the buildout without quarterly earnings calls. In the "Old Macro" world, a company with 8x debt was a "sell." In the "New AI Macro" world, that same company is a "must-buy" because its power plants are the most valuable assets on earth.

Where the Smart Money Flows

The power sector has seen more than $280 billion in M&A announced since the start of 2025. It is becoming evident that energy is a chokepoint in the ongoing hypergrowth of AI. While many, including me, debate whether OpenAI and Anthropic can IPO this year, as discussed in my February 10 article, the private equity firms, with their billions of dry powder, are moving fast to capitalize on the need.

Specifically for AES, the conflict in Iran has made the case for domestic renewable generation capacity impossible to ignore. This trade will keep the arbitrage desks busy as they manage the position. Given the 100% equity financing for the take-private, the $588 million reverse break fee, and GIP's track record of clearing regulatory hurdles at ALLETE (ALE), the smart money has good reason to sit on this one. I would not bet against them.
data.author: Omer Shenhar

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/24/26 – Stock Futures Slip as U.S.-Iran Resolution Hopes Fade
data.description:
U.S. stock futures slipped early Tuesday after major indexes rebounded Monday, fueled by President Donald Trump's comments on "productive talks" with Iran that eased geopolitical tensions. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.17%, 0.23%, and 0.28%, respectively, at 5:48 a.m. EST on March 24.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

At the time of writing, the global oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was down 9.2% at $102.19 per barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) fell 7.45% to $91.23 per barrel. Oil prices fell sharply due to profit-taking and hopes for U.S.-Iran de-escalation.

During Monday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed higher amid renewed hopes for an imminent resolution to the U.S.-Iran standoff. The Dow and the Nasdaq each gained 1.38%, while the S&P 500 surged 1.15%.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield rose to near 4.37%. Additionally, the Spot Gold U.S. dollar price hovered around $4,407 per ounce on Tuesday.

Looking ahead, traders await the S&P Global Services PMI (Purchasing Managers Index) report, a key gauge of service-sector health. On the earnings front, companies reporting today include GameStop (GME), PaySign (PAYS), Vertical Aerospace (EVTL), and KB Home (KBH).

Elsewhere, European indexes opened lower today amid lingering caution over the U.S.-Iran talks, despite positive signals.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Higher

Asia-Pacific markets traded higher on March 24, tracking Wall Street's rebound.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index surged 2.79%. In China, the Shanghai Composite gained 1.78%, while the Shenzhen Component increased 2.17%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei rose 1.43% and the Topix added 2.10%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Is Cathie Wood Turning Bearish on Bullish (BLSH) Stock?
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made a few small but notable portfolio adjustments on Monday, March 23, as shown in ARK's daily fund disclosures. The famed growth‑thematic manager sold a total of 39,362 shares of cryptocurrency exchange Bullish (BLSH) for $1.56 million.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade ARKK with leverage
  • New trading tool for ARKK bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade ARKK

These shares were offloaded from the ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW) and the ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK), likely taking advantage of BLSH's 4.1% stock‑price advance on the day. This sale followed ARK's earlier disposal of 103,379 BLSH shares on Friday, March 20, valued at about $3.93 million.

Meanwhile, on the buy side, ARK added 98,722 shares of 10X Genomics (TXG) for $1.89 million, even as that stock rose 4.08%.

These moves naturally spark a central question: is Wood truly turning bearish on Bullish, or is this just another tactical shuffle? Despite the recent selling, Wood has also been one of BLSH's most aggressive buyers over the past year. Let's briefly understand Wood's stance on Bullish stock.

Wood's BLSH Moves Are Tactical, Not Definitive

ARK has been very open about its bullish stance on Bullish, buying tens of millions of dollars' worth of shares in late 2025 and early 2026. Wood purchased roughly $12 million in November 2025 and more multi‑million‑dollar buys around earnings and price dips. Against this backdrop, a multi‑day reduction can look like a sentiment shift, even though ARK likely still holds a sizable stake.

In February, ARK poured more money into BLSH, buying about 2.1 million shares worth roughly $58–$60 million over nine straight days. These buys came after BLSH's post-earnings pullback, and ARK also picked up Robinhood (HOOD) and other crypto‑linked stocks. Even as ARK trimmed BLSH in late March 2026, it kept investing in other crypto‑related names.

What Is Driving Wood's Portfolio Choices?

Three key factors help explain why ARK is scaling back on BLSH while still holding a long-term bullish conviction.

  • Valuation and Volatility: After a strong post‑IPO run, BLSH fell about 27–50% from its peak, giving ARK a chance to buy cheap and cash in on price rallies. Buying on lows and selling on highs is normal for volatile, high‑growth stocks like BLSH.
  • Portfolio Rotation: ARK has been steadily increasing its biotech exposures, like 10x Genomics, while selling some fintech and crypto‑linked names. This is mainly about balancing risk and sector mix, not branding BLSH as a bad stock.
  • Crypto‑market swings: Crypto‑linked stocks like BLSH often swing sharply with Bitcoin (BTC-USD) prices and regulatory news. ARK may be cutting back on BLSH to lower risk and keep it as a smaller, leveraged bet on long‑term crypto adoption instead of a big core holding.

Overall, ARK's recent trades look like a tactical reshuffle of its crypto and biotech bets, not a full rejection of Bullish.

Is BLSH Stock Good to Buy?

Analysts remain divided on Bullish's long-term outlook. On TipRanks, BLSH has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on four Buys and two Hold ratings. The average Bullish price target of $47.83 implies 20.9% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Says AGI Is Here, But Admits AI Still Can't Build a Company Like Nvidia
data.description:
NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) Chief Executive Jensen Huang said this week that AI has already reached artificial general intelligence, or AGI. However, his view of what counts as AGI is not the one many in the tech world use.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade NVDA with leverage
  • New trading tool for NVDA bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade NVDA

Speaking on the Lex Fridman Podcast, Huang said, "I think it's now. I think we've achieved AGI." The comment came after a question about whether AI could build and run a company worth over $1 billion.

Huang did not focus on long-term business success. Instead, he pointed to AI's ability to create a product that could go viral and bring in large revenue, even if only for a short time. "You mentioned a billion, and you didn't specify forever," he said.

Meanwhile, NVDA shares rose 1.70% on Monday, closing at $175.64.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Amazon (AMZN) Flags AWS Outage in Bahrain — How Customers and Investors Are Affected
data.description:
E-commerce giant Amazon (AMZN) announced that its cloud computing unit, Amazon Web Services (AWS) faced a disruption in its Bahrain region due to drone activity linked to the ongoing Middle East conflict. Amazon added that it is redirecting customers to other AWS regions while it works to resolve the issue. However, it did not share when services will be fully restored.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, after weeks of conflict, President Trump yesterday announced a five-day delay in planned strikes on Iran's energy infrastructure, easing immediate fears of a major supply disruption.

What the AWS Outage Means for Amazon Customers

The disruption in AWS's Bahrain region could temporarily slow services for some customers in the Middle East. AWS also stated that the attacks caused power outages, structural damage, and even water shortages due to fire-control measures. As a result, several cloud services were disrupted, and full recovery could take longer than initially expected.

This is the second time AWS's Bahrain region has been hit by drones since the U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran began. Earlier this month, AWS said its facilities in Bahrain and the United Arab Emirates were affected by power outages and that it was working to restore services.

Interestingly, global tech companies like Amazon are investing heavily in the UAE for AI and cloud computing, making the region more important than ever.

What Amazon Investors Need to Know

AWS powers many popular websites and government operations and is also the company's main profit driver. In Q4 2025, AWS generated $35.6 billion in revenue, growing 24% year-over-year — its fastest growth in 13 quarters.

But this is a regional issue, and it's unlikely to affect Amazon's global cloud business or long-term growth. However, investors should expect some short-term pressure on AWS revenue in the Middle East. Overall, the company's quick move to shift customers to other regions should help limit losses, keeping Amazon's overall profit outlook largely intact.

Here's a look at AWS's performance over the past few quarters, showing how the business has grown.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: 'No More Sports Bets' – New Bill Targets Kalshi, Polymarket
data.description:
U.S. senators have introduced a bipartisan bill to prohibit sports betting on prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket. This legislation targets the blurring line between financial trading platforms and gambling. Introduced on March 23, 2026, by Democratic Sen. Adam Schiff of California and Republican Sen. John Curtis of Utah, the bill amends the Commodity Exchange Act.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

It bans contracts settling on sports events or casino-style games, like poker or blackjack, from platforms regulated by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC). Prediction markets treat these trades as "event contracts," bypassing state-licensed sportsbooks like FanDuel (FLUT) or DraftKings (DKNG).

Sports Betting Risks Manipulation

The bill addresses concerns that sports predictions on Kalshi and Polymarket act as unregulated, nationwide bets. Traditional sports betting has adhered to state laws since the 2018 Supreme Court ruling, but CFTC oversight allows prediction markets to skirt these rules. Kalshi's Super Bowl trading hit over $1 billion this year, up 2700% year-over-year, fueling the debate.

Lawmakers argue these are "sports bets, just with a different name," violating state and federal laws. The bill spares state-regulated operators while forcing platforms to drop sports and casino markets for CFTC compliance. Polymarket, which operates in a U.S. gray area, would also halt such offerings.

This first Senate bipartisan effort signals rising scrutiny of prediction markets' $4.5 billion weekly volumes. Popularity on these platforms has soared for politics, weather, and sports trading, but sports bets now dominate. If the bill passes, it redraws lines between innovation and gambling.

Kalshi Blocks Insider Trading to Curb Manipulation

Facing strict rules, Kalshi is tightening measures against insider trading and manipulation in political and sports markets. It now proactively bars athletes, officials, and politicians from relevant trades, which were previously banned but not always enforced.

Kalshi will prohibit political candidates from trading contracts tied to their own campaigns. It will also preemptively bar anyone affiliated with college or professional sports (players or staff) from betting on markets related to the leagues or teams they are involved with.

The changes align with CFTC guidance and pending congressional bills targeting sports-related event contracts for CFTC-regulated firms. Kalshi is also launching a whistleblower feature for users to report suspicious activity early, aiming to standardize practices with regulators and stakeholders as its valuation doubled to $11 billion in December 2025.

Is Kalshi Publicly Traded?

Kalshi is a private company with no announced plans for an IPO (initial public offering). Check TipRanks' Private Companies Center for updates on similar firms eyeing public markets.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: 'Own Nvidia, Broadcom,' But Avoid AMD Stock – Bernstein Weighs In on AI Chip Stocks
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA), Broadcom (AVGO), and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) remain key players in the AI chip space, but Bernstein is drawing a clear line between the winners and the laggards.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade NVDA with leverage
  • New trading tool for NVDA bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade NVDA

Top analyst Stacy Rasgon said AI demand is still strong, but not all stocks are benefiting equally. While Nvidia and Broadcom continue to see solid demand and attractive valuations, the firm is less convinced about AMD, citing the need for clearer signs of sustained customer demand.

Rising AI Demand Is Driving New Challenges

Rasgon said AI demand remains strong, but it is now creating new challenges. A sharp rise in demand is putting pressure on memory supply, pushing prices higher and creating concerns for other parts of the tech market.

As data centers take up more capacity, sectors like PCs and smartphones are starting to feel the impact. Rising component costs are also beginning to show up as a headwind for margins.

Bernstein Backs Nvidia, Broadcom; Stays Cautious on AMD

Despite these issues, Bernstein remains positive on Nvidia and Broadcom. The firm believes demand trends remain intact, and current estimates for both companies may still be too low.

Rasgon noted that both stocks could be trading at around 15x or less based on longer-term earnings, suggesting room for upside if AI demand holds at current levels.

For AMD, Bernstein is taking a more cautious stance. While the stock has rebounded from recent lows, the firm wants clearer proof that customers are choosing AMD's chips out of real demand, not just limited supply elsewhere. For now, Bernstein prefers to stay on the sidelines until there is more consistent evidence of growth.

Which Chip Stock Is the Best Buy Right Now? 

Turning to Wall Street, out of the three stocks mentioned above, Nvidia offers the highest upside at about 56% and carries a Strong Buy rating from analysts. Broadcom follows with roughly 45% upside and also holds a Strong Buy consensus.

AMD offers around 41% upside but has a more cautious Moderate Buy rating compared to its peers.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
